hook.text: On January 28, 1986, the Space Shuttle Challenger broke apart 73 seconds after launch.

buildup.text: Engineers at Morton Thiokol warned that cold weather could cause the O-ring seals to fail.

discovery.text: Managers overruled the engineers. The launch proceeded despite temperatures below the design limit.

twist.text: The O-rings failed exactly as predicted. Seven crew members died on live television.

climax.text: The Rogers Commission found that NASA's culture silenced the people who knew the danger.

punchline.text: The engineers were right. The managers launched anyway.

quiz.question: Why did the Challenger disaster happen?
options: Managers overruled engineers who warned about cold weather O-ring failure
A bird struck the shuttle during launch
The fuel tanks were filled incorrectly
